questions.question: 1 (a) What is the equilibrium constant expression for this reaction? parts.answer: The only correct answer is A (rate = k[NO] [O ]; rate = k[NO] [O ]) B is incorrect because the rate equation is not determined by the stoichiometric equation C is incorrect because the rate cannot depend only on the concentration of products D is incorrect because the rate cannot depend only on the concentration of products (1) number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is B(colorimetry; volume change) A is incorrect because titration cannot be used for continuous monitoring of a reaction C is incorrect because the mass of the system does not change D is incorrect because the mass of the system does not change and titration cannot be used for continuous monitoring of a reaction (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is C (the time taken for the concentration of a reactant to halve) A is incorrect because the reaction is slower and slower as it progresses B is incorrect because the rate constant does not change at a given temperature D is incorrect because time taken for the concentration of a product to double will vary (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is A (rate = k[E][F]) B is incorrect because the rate depends on concentrations of the reacting species in the slow step and the stoichiometry of this step C is incorrect because the rate equation cannot include intermediate concentrations D is incorrect because the rate depends on concentrations of the reacting species in the slow step and the stoichiometry of this step and the rate equation cannot include intermediate concentrations (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is D (graph 4) A is incorrect because entropy increases with temperature and the entropy change for the change from liquid to gas is greater than that for solid to liquid B is incorrect because entropy increases with temperature C is incorrect because the entropy change for the change from liquid to gas is greater than that for solid to liquid (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is C (increases; unchanged) A is incorrect because the energy of the system does not change for an ideal gas B is incorrect because the entropy of the system increases and the energy of the system does not change for an ideal gas D is incorrect because the entropy of the system increases (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is B (kinetically stable and thermodynamica

The only correct answer is D(K c unchanged; K c decreases) A is incorrect because K c is not affected by the state of division of the solid and K c decreases for an exothermic reaction B is incorrect because K c decreases for an exothermic reaction C is incorrect because K c is not affected by the state of division of the solid (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is A(HCOOH, HCOO − , CH COOH + , CH COOH) B is incorrect because methanoic acid is the stronger acid and will protonate ethanoic acid C is incorrect because the acids and bases have been paired incorrectly D is incorrect because methanoic acid is the stronger acid and the acids and bases have been paired incorrectly (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is A(increases, increases) B is incorrect because the solution becomes less acidic with dilution so pH increases C is incorrect because the proportion of acid molecules dissociating increases with dilution D is incorrect because the proportion of acid molecules dissociating increases with dilution and the solution becomes less acidic with dilution so pH increases (1) number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is C (10.3) A is incorrect because the concentration of OH − ions has been obtained by halving the concentration of magnesium hydroxide rather than doubling it B is incorrect because the concentration of OH − ions has been taken as equal to the concentration of magnesium hydroxide D is incorrect because the concentration of OH − ions has been obtained by taking the square root of the concentration of magnesium hydroxide (1) number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is B(higher; higher) A is incorrect because the solution at 100°C will be more alkaline C is incorrect because the concentration of OH − ions at 100°C will be higher as solubility is greater D is incorrect because the concentration of OH − ions at 100°C will be higher and the solution will be more alkaline (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is B(propanal forms permanent dipole-permanent dipole forces) A is incorrect because the London forces in the two molecules will be similar C is incorrect because pure propanal does not form hydrogen bonds D is the alcohol needs to be on the C3 atom C is incorrect because the acyl chloride needs to have four carbon atoms and the methyl group of the alcohol needs to be on the C3 atom D is incorrect because the acyl chloride needs to have four carbon atoms (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is A (ethanal and propanone) B is incorrect because propanone also has the CH CO group that results in a positive iodoform test C is incorrect because ethanal also has the CH CO group that results in a positive iodoform test D is incorrect because both ethanal and propanone have the CH CO group that results in a positive iodoform test (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is C (0.70) A is incorrect because the distance travelled by X has been measured from the solvent front B is
